channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Creature Commandos season 1 ending explained: who dies, is there a post-credits scene, will there be a season 2, and more of your biggest questions answered
item.description: Creature Commandos' season 1 finale is out now – here's what happens in it and whether it sets up future DCU projects.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Amazon's $11 Billion Investment in Georgia for AI-Enabled Data Centers
item.description: Amazon Web Services (AWS) plans to invest $11 billion in Georgia to support the growing demand for generative AI and other cloud technologies.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta tries letting Facebook Marketplace users view eBay listings as way to resolve EU charges
item.description: Meta said Wednesday that it will allow some Facebook users to view eBay listings on its Marketplace service, as it tries out a possible way to resolve European Union charges of anticompetitive behavior that the bloc leveled last year.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Why I’m making 2025 my year of dead-tree books
item.description: Over the past 17 years or so, I have happily paid for hundreds of e-books—everything from reference works to history to novels to comics. Having immediate access to all of them on a device that fits in my pocket ranks among my favorite miracles of the digital age.
item.dc:creator: Harry McCracken, Fast Company

item.title: Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025
item.description: AI in action: How agencies are building out trusted artificial intelligence capabilities The post Federal News Network’s AI & Data Exchange 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: wfedstaff, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft is finally fighting back against the rise of SteamOS by overhauling Windows 11 to be better on handhelds
item.description: Windows 11 is often cited as the worst thing about handheld gaming PCs - but it looks like Microsoft could soon fix this.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: In the social media wars, Bluesky is destroying Truth Social
item.description: In November 2024, a left-leaning cohort of social users ran to Bluesky. The movement made the platform a liberal haven—and more popular than far-right alternative Truth Social. According to a new report from The Righting, Bluesky’s November user share toppled Truth Social’s.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: New League of Legends video might have set up Arcane's sequel series, and now I'm even more desperate to see the hit Netflix show's first follow-up
item.description: A new trailer for Riot Games' hugely popular game series appears to act as a prologue to one of Arcane's sequel projects.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Nearly a Quarter of Freshwater Animals Face Extinction Threat
item.description: New research reveals alarming decline in freshwater species due to pollution, dams, and climate change.
item.dc:creator: Christina Larson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: ByteDance's Lemon8 gains traction amid TikTok ban threat as creators push the app
item.description: Hearing a lot about Lemon8 lately? You’re not the only one. Amid a looming U.S. ban on TikTok, content creators have been pushing the platform's sister app.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Preventing this insidious email forwarding scam that will drain your bank account
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says business email compromise scams are a serious threat, as shown by Teresa W.'s near loss of thousands of dollars.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oracle offers price-performance boost with Exadata X11M update
item.description: The new version of Oracle’s Exadata platform, X11M, is now generally available, and promises improved performance on generative AI tasks such as retrieval augmented generation (RAG) for the same price as the old version.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A 750Hz gaming monitor has come out of nowhere at CES 2025 – but I’m getting a bit sick of this refresh rate one-upmanship
item.description: Want a gaming monitor for silky smooth frame rates? There’s a new 750Hz panel in town – but how high is too high for refresh rates?
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Egypt unveils ancient rock-cut tombs and burial shafts in Luxor
item.description: Egypt has unveiled several discoveries near the famed city of Luxor including ancient rock-cut tombs and burial shafts dating back 3,600 years.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Japan links Chinese hacker MirrorFace to dozens of cyberattacks targeting security and tech data
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japan on Wednesday linked more than 200 cyberattacks over the past five years targeting the country's national security and high technology data to a Chinese hacking group, MirrorFace, detailing their tactics and calling on government agencies and businesses to reinforce preventive meas
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Man used ChatGPT to plan Las Vegas Cybertruck blast
item.description: The man killed in the Las Vegas Cybertruck blast used ChatGPT to plan the explosion, police said. In a press conference, Tuesday, Las Vegas police released more details of the intentions of 37-year-old Matthew Livelsberger, who died of a gunshot wound prior to the car exploding.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, The Hill

item.title: Flying taxis are on the horizon as aviation soars into a new frontier
item.description: When he was still a boy making the long, tedious trips between his school and his woodsy home in the mountains during the 1980s, JoeBen Bevirt began fantasizing about flying cars that could whisk him to his destination in a matter of minutes.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Meta looking for 'sweetheart deal' with Trump: FTC chair
item.description: Outgoing Federal Trade Commission (FTC) Chair Lina Khan suggested Tuesday that Meta and other companies facing antitrust lawsuits from the agency might be looking for “sweetheart deals” from the incoming Trump administration.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Why Apple’s AI-driven reality distortion matters
item.description: Apple has been forced to admit what every company involved in artificial intelligence (AI) should also be forced to state — AI makes mistakes, just like people do. On the surface, it’s not a terribly big deal: Apple’s AI badly mangled a handful of news headlines.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities
item.description: The White House is prepping a new cybersecurity executive order that would address cloud security, give CISA new authorities to do threat hunting and more. The post White House new cyber EO would give CISA more authorities first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: How adding capacity to a network could reduce IT costs
item.description: Finding an enterprise looking to spend more on network equipment is like finding gold while digging for worms for bait. Cost management used to be an option in enhancing profits, but these days it’s a mantra.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Meta's new board includes UFC boss Dana White, a familiar figure in Trump's orbit
item.description: Meta has appointed three new members to its board of directors, including Dana White, the president and CEO of Ultimate Fighting Championship and a familiar figure in the orbit of incoming President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Keep yourself and your money safe from these four scams
item.description: Authorities are warning people to be wary of pig butchering scams, travel scams, law enforcement scams and utility scams to prevent losing money to fraudsters.
item.dc:creator: Nollyanne Delacruz, The Mercury News

item.title: CES 2025: Nvidia Unveils GeForce RTX 50 Series, AMD Showcases Ryzen 9 and AI Processors
item.description: Nvidia and AMD announce new GPUs and processors at CES 2025, highlighting advancements in artificial intelligence for PC gaming and content creation.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Agentic AI: The top challenges and how to overcome them
item.description: As generative AI continues to surge in popularity, we are already seeing it evolve into the next generation of machine-learning-driven technology: agentic AI. With agentic AI, we are not just prompting models and receiving an answer in a simple one-step process.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: With o3 having reached AGI, OpenAI turns its sights toward superintelligence
item.description: OpenAI CEO Sam Altman has reinvigorated discussion of artificial general intelligence (AGI), boldly claiming that his company’s newest model has reached that milestone.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Fubo combining with Disney's Hulu + Live TV; lawsuit against Venu Sports settled
item.description: Disney's Hulu + Live TV and sports streaming service Fubo are combining in a deal that will also see the settlement of a lawsuit against the creation of Venu Sports.
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE CHAPMAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Planes, trains and third-party risks
item.description: Christmas Eve (and Christmas Day) are arguably the most important time-frame for transportation companies. So it was a big deal when an American Airlines system glitch forced the airline to ask the government for a full shutdown on Christmas Eve.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Easy internet sleuth tricks you’ll wish you knew sooner
item.description: Here are some powerful online sleuthing tricks you can use to check the credibility of a website, see old pictures of places you've lived and even learn more about your neighbors.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024
item.description: Current and former federal technology executives weighed in on what topics drove the storylines in 2024. The post No surprise: AI, cyber continued to dominate in 2024 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Microsoft raises bar with water-saving data center design
item.description: Microsoft’s new data center designs include a cooling system that it claims will consume zero water while at the same time offering liquid cooling of the hardware. The designs leverage a chip-level liquid cooling solution that can provide precise temperature control without water evaporation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Confronting the growing drone threat
item.description: There is a solid rationale for prioritizing safe, secure and controlled RF cyber-based counter-UAS technology as decision makers consider future standards. The post Confronting the growing drone threat first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network
